step_by_step_thinking:
Clinical Reasoning:
Post-streptococcal glomerulonephritis can manifest with both nephrotic and nephritic syndromes, making it unique among the listed conditions since its hallmark is primarily nephritic signs rather than purely nephrotic ones.
\boxed{B} Based on the information provided, only Post-streptococcal Glomerulonephritis (PSGN) predominantly manifests with features of the nephritic syndrome, which can occasionally resemble nephrotic syndrome through coincidental proteinuria without being the defining feature. Other forms mentioned—minimal change disease, focal segmental glomerulosclerosis (FSGS), and membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis—are known to prominently display characteristics of the nephrotic syndrome. Thus,
